title: Preventing Opioid Overdose | Overdose Prevention | CDC
text:

Signs of an overdose may include:
Unconsciousness or inability to awaken Slow or shallow breathing or difficulty breathing such as choking sounds or gurgling/snoring noise from a person who cannot be awakened Discolored skin (especially in nails or lips) Small, constricted "pinpoint pupils" that don't react to light 
Find treatment for substance use disorder, including opioid use disorder 
If you or someone close to you needs help for a substance use disorder, talk to your doctor or call SAMHSA's National Helpline at 1-800-662-HELP or go to SAMHSA's Behavioral Health Treatment Services Locator
